chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ يَشْتَرِي الرَّقِيقَ فَيَظْهَرُ بِهِ عَيْبٌ وَ مَا يُرَدُّ مِنْهُ وَ مَا لَا يُرَدُّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 94 – The one who buys the slave so a defact is evident with him, and what is returnable from it, and what is not returnable

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالحسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/245)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالحسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/245)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ بَاعَ جَارِيَةً عَلَى أَنَّهَا بِكْرٌ فَلَمْ يَجِدْهَا عَلَى ذَلِكَ قَالَ لَا تُرَدُّ عَلَيْهِ وَ لَا يُوجَبُ عَلَيْهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ إِنَّهُ يَكُونُ يَذْهَبُ فِي حَالِ مَرَضٍ أَوْ أَمْرٍ يُصِيبُهَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from the one who narrated it, from Zurara Bin Muhammad, from Sama’at who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about a man who sold a slave girl upon a stipulation that she was a virgin, but he (the buyer) did not find her to be upon that. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He cannot return her to him, and nothing would be obligated upon it, it (virginity) can happen to go away during a state of illness or a matter which had affected her’.
en.sarwar: 10. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Jamil ibn Darraj from certain persons of our people who has said the following: “About the case of a man who buys a slave-girl and makes her to give birth, then finds her to be stolen, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Her owner takes the slave-girl and the man takes the child.’”
